hero.headline: Chandigarh Online MBA in Brand Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme with Harvard and PwC certification options

sections.tldr: Chandigarh Online MBA in Brand Management covers brand strategy, consumer behaviour, integrated marketing communications, digital brand building, brand equity measurement, PwC-taught modules (premium tier), zero-cost direct university EMI, and 25% Early Bird scholarship at Rs 1,65,000. NIRF #19, NAAC A+. For brand management, marketing, and consumer insights careers.

about.body:
Brand Management is a specialised MBA track for professionals aspiring to brand manager, marketing manager, and consumer insights roles in FMCG, D2C, retail, and services companies. India's organised consumer sector is creating strong demand for trained brand managers who understand both classical brand strategy and digital brand building across social media, e-commerce, and influencer platforms.

Chandigarh University Online's Brand Management MBA covers brand strategy and architecture, consumer behaviour, integrated marketing communications, digital brand building, brand equity measurement, and product and price management. The programme prepares graduates for the full brand management function from strategy to execution.

For Tier 2 enrollees, PwC's Consumer Markets advisory practice advises FMCG companies, retail chains, and D2C brands on commercial transformation, brand valuation, and consumer experience design. Co-taught modules bring advisory-grade brand and consumer frameworks into the curriculum.

Chandigarh University holds NIRF #19 overall and NIRF #32 in Management 2025, NAAC A+ accredited. EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all Chandigarh MBA specialisations. See the main Chandigarh Online MBA page for core subject list.

subjects.name: Strategy, Business and Globalization
subjects.desc: Brand strategy in competitive markets, global brand portfolio management, and brand-led competitive positioning.

subjects.name: Decision Science
subjects.desc: Marketing analytics for brand tracking, share of voice measurement, and consumer panel data analysis.

subjects.name: Brand Strategy and Architecture
subjects.desc: Brand positioning frameworks, brand architecture models (branded house vs house of brands), brand extension strategy.

subjects.name: Consumer Behaviour and Insights
subjects.desc: Consumer psychology, buying behaviour models, qualitative and quantitative research methods for brand insights.

subjects.name: Integrated Marketing Communications
subjects.desc: IMC planning, above-the-line and below-the-line integration, media mix optimisation, and campaign effectiveness measurement.

subjects.name: Digital Brand Building
subjects.desc: Social media brand strategy, influencer marketing architecture, content marketing for brand equity, and D2C brand community building.

subjects.name: Project Management
subjects.desc: Brand campaign project management, product launch management, and agency and vendor coordination.

subjects.name: AI for Managers
subjects.desc: AI in consumer sentiment analysis, personalised brand communication, programmatic advertising for brand management.

subjects.name: Brand Equity Measurement
subjects.desc: Brand equity models (Aaker, Keller), brand valuation methods, brand health tracker design, and NPS for brands.

subjects.name: Product and Price Management
subjects.desc: Product lifecycle management, pricing strategy for premium and value brands, pack architecture, and portfolio rationalisation.

subjects.name: Capstone Project II
subjects.desc: Applied brand management project: brand repositioning plan, new brand launch strategy, or brand equity improvement programme.

whoHires.body:
Brand Management MBA graduates from Chandigarh University Online are hired across India's consumer goods and services sectors:

**Industries:** FMCG, FMCD, D2C brands, retail, pharmaceuticals, financial services, hospitality, and advertising agencies

**Entry-level roles:** Assistant Brand Manager, Marketing Executive, Consumer Insights Analyst, Digital Marketing Executive, Product Manager (junior)

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Brand Manager, Senior Brand Manager, Consumer Insights Manager, Marketing Manager, Digital Brand Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Marketing Director, VP Marketing, Chief Marketing Officer, Head of Brand, VP Consumer Insights

**Top hiring organisations:** HUL, P&G, ITC, Nestle, Marico, Dabur, Godrej Consumer Products, Britannia, Parle, Emami, Asian Paints, Titan, Tata Consumer Products, Nykaa, Mamaearth, Sugar Cosmetics, and D2C brands across beauty, food, and personal care categories

skills.body:
The Brand Management specialisation builds skills that FMCG and consumer goods employers test at brand manager level:

**Technical skills:**
- Brand Strategy: brand positioning statement construction, category entry point mapping, brand architecture audit, and competitive brand repositioning
- Consumer Research: qualitative discussion guide design, focus group moderation basics, usage and attitude study interpretation, and brand equity tracker analysis
- IMC Planning: media brief writing, agency evaluation criteria, campaign effectiveness metrics (reach, frequency, GRP), and message hierarchy development
- Brand Equity: Aaker's brand equity model application, brand valuation methodology basics, and brand health scorecard design

**Applied skills:**
- Digital Brand Building: social content calendar planning, influencer brief development, community management strategy, and D2C brand storytelling
- Product and Pricing: SKU rationalisation analysis, price-volume trade-off modelling, pack size architecture, and competitive price positioning

**Soft skills:** Presenting brand plans and P&L to marketing leadership and sales teams, managing creative agency relationships under campaign deadline pressure, defending brand investment decisions to finance stakeholders, and communicating consumer insights to cross-functional teams in actionable language.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Chandigarh Online MBA Brand Management against peer programmes:

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Marketing Management:** NMIMS has strong marketing brand with FMCG hiring connections, particularly in Mumbai. CU's dedicated Brand Management specialisation has Brand Equity Measurement and Brand Architecture as standalone subjects that NMIMS Marketing Management does not offer at this depth in online programmes. CU Tier 2 adds PwC Consumer Markets advisory. Fee: CU Rs 1,65,000 Tier 1 vs NMIMS higher fee.

**vs Amity Online MBA Marketing:** Amity's Marketing programme is well-known. CU's Brand Management specialisation is more focused for candidates specifically targeting brand manager roles, with dedicated consumer behaviour, brand equity, and digital brand building subjects. NIRF #19 gives CU a ranking advantage.

**vs LPU Online MBA Marketing:** LPU has NAAC A++ at lower fee. CU's advantage is NIRF #19, dedicated brand management curriculum depth, PwC Consumer Markets advisory (Tier 2), and Harvard Business Publishing certificate. For FMCG brand manager roles where credentials are screened, CU's profile is stronger.

fees.body:
The Chandigarh University Online MBA in Brand Management offers 2 program tiers based on certification bundle:

**Tier 1 (Primary) - Standard with Harvard ManageMentor Certificate**:
- Actual program fee: Rs 2,20,000
- 25% Early Bird Scholarship: Rs 1,65,000 net payable (Rs 55,000 savings)
- Payment options:
  - Annual: Rs 82,500 per year x 2 years
  - Semester-wise: Rs 41,250 per semester x 4 semesters
  - Full upfront: Rs 1,65,000 one-time
- Included certification: Harvard Business Publishing certificate

**Tier 2 (Premium Upgrade) - 3-Certificate Bundle**:
- Actual program fee: Rs 2,20,000 (same base fee)
- 18% Early Bird Scholarship: Rs 1,80,400 net payable (Rs 39,600 savings)
- Payment options:
  - Annual: Rs 90,200 per year x 2 years
  - Semester-wise: Rs 45,100 per semester x 4 semesters
  - Full upfront: Rs 1,80,400 one-time
- Included certifications (3):
  1. PwC co-created and co-taught MBA modules (unmatched in India)
  2. CAPM (Certified Associate in Project Management) certification preparation
  3. Harvard Business Publishing certificate

**Additional charges (both tiers)**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time, non-refundable)

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Chandigarh University (no external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary, a differentiator among Indian online MBAs). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options (6, 12, 18, or 24 months).

**Early Bird Scholarship terms**: Availability varies by admission cycle. The 25% Tier 1 scholarship and 18% Tier 2 scholarship are currently active but subject to timing. Reconfirm eligibility with our counsellor before enrolment.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Chandigarh University each quarter. Please reconfirm current tier options, Early Bird eligibility, and fees with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive a Chandigarh University MBA degree certificate with Brand Management noted on the marksheet, issued under UGC-DEB 2020 standards. NIRF #19 and NAAC A+ credentials are recognised by FMCG companies, D2C brands, and advertising agencies.

Tier 1 graduates receive the Harvard Business Publishing certificate. Tier 2 graduates additionally receive PwC Consumer Markets advisory co-teaching acknowledgement and CAPM certification preparation, strengthening profiles for brand strategy consulting, FMCG brand management, and consumer insights leadership. DigiLocker verification is available.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Chandigarh University Online MBA Brand Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Ananya S.
items.liked: Brand Strategy and Consumer Behaviour subjects were directly applicable to my FMCG brand manager role
items.disliked: Brand valuation financial modelling needed more quantitative depth
items.body: I work as a brand executive at an FMCG company and the Brand Strategy and Architecture subject covered positioning frameworks, brand architecture decisions, and extension strategy that my work requires daily. Consumer Behaviour's qualitative research methods improved my briefing of research agencies. PwC Consumer Markets modules (Tier 2) brought commercial frameworks that I used in my brand P&L review presentations. Harvard Business Publishing certificate was noted by my promotion committee.

items.name: Ravi M.
items.liked: Digital Brand Building covered D2C community building and influencer marketing architecture
items.disliked: Brand licensing and co-branding strategy could have a dedicated module
items.body: I manage D2C brand marketing and the Digital Brand Building subject covered community building strategy and influencer brief development that I apply directly. IMC Planning gave me the above-the-line frameworks I was missing from my digital-only background. NAAC A+ from NIRF #19 CU satisfied the management degree requirement for brand manager roles. Zero-cost EMI through CU was convenient.

items.name: Sunita K.
items.liked: Brand Equity Measurement and product lifecycle management content directly applicable
items.disliked: B2B brand management needed a dedicated module
items.body: I work in product management at a consumer electronics company. Brand Equity Measurement covered Aaker's model and brand health tracker design that my marketing team now uses. Product and Price Management's SKU rationalisation tools helped me build the case for our portfolio reduction project. Good programme for working marketing and brand management professionals wanting credentials.

faqs.question: What is the PwC partnership in Chandigarh Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Chandigarh University's premium tier includes full MBA modules co-created and co-taught by PwC industry experts. This is unmatched among Indian online MBAs - most offer only generic curriculum. PwC-taught modules are particularly valuable for Brand Management professionals seeking roles at FMCG companies, brand consulting firms, or consumer goods companies that work with PwC Consumer Markets advisory. The PwC partnership is included in Tier 2 (premium) at Rs 1,80,400 net.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Tier 1 and Tier 2 fee options?
faqs.answer: Both tiers share the same Rs 2,20,000 base fee and identical curriculum. Tier 1 applies a 25% Early Bird Scholarship at Rs 1,65,000 net with the Harvard Business Publishing certificate. Tier 2 applies an 18% Early Bird Scholarship at Rs 1,80,400 net with three certifications: Harvard, CAPM preparation, and PwC co-taught modules. For brand management professionals targeting FMCG, D2C leadership, or brand consulting, Tier 2 adds strong credentialing value.

faqs.question: Is zero-cost EMI really available directly through Chandigarh University?
faqs.answer: Yes. Chandigarh University offers zero-cost EMI through the university's own payment infrastructure, not through an NBFC or external marketing partner. Tenures of 6, 12, 18, and 24 months are typically available. Confirm current options with our counsellor before enrolment.

faqs.question: Is this MBA useful for HUL or P&G brand manager applications?
faqs.answer: Management degree requirements for brand manager roles at large FMCG companies specify a recognised MBA. The Chandigarh University MBA in Brand Management from a UGC-DEB entitled, NAAC A+ university satisfies this requirement. The Brand Management specialisation covers the specific skills these companies test: brand positioning, consumer research, IMC planning, and brand equity measurement.

faqs.question: Does the programme cover digital and D2C brand building specifically?
faqs.answer: Yes. Digital Brand Building in Sem 3 specifically covers D2C brand community building, influencer marketing architecture, content marketing for brand equity, and social media brand strategy. This subject is directly applicable for professionals at D2C brands like Mamaearth, Sugar, and BoAt, as well as FMCG companies' digital marketing functions managing their DTC channels.

chooseIf: You work in FMCG, D2C, or consumer goods marketing and want a NIRF #19, NAAC A+ MBA with dedicated brand management curriculum.
You want PwC Consumer Markets advisory content (Tier 2) for brand strategy or consumer goods consulting roles.
You are targeting FMCG brand manager, consumer insights, or D2C brand leadership roles.
You prefer zero-cost EMI directly through the university.
consider: You need IIM or XLRI brand specifically for HUL, P&G, or top FMCG brand management roles where top-tier MBA brand matters.
You are targeting advertising and creative agency careers where portfolio and creative credentials matter more.
Your budget is under Rs 1.2 lakh and a lower-fee NAAC A++ alternative is acceptable.
